title: Agentic Persona Manifest
description: The single index-of-record for one agentic persona. It ties together the persona's identity, the definition layer(s) that implement it, the skills it uses, its trigger surface, its trust/permission posture, its eval gate, and a pointer to its canary-rings entry. The manifest does NOT duplicate ring membership or gate knobs — those live in petry-projects/.github standards/canary-rings.json (the single source of truth). See standards/persona-standards.md.

then.description: A persona at status 'stable' MUST carry an evals block (the held-out gate). See persona-standards.md §1.

schema_version.description: Manifest schema version. Bump only when the schema shape changes.

id.description: Stable kebab-case identifier. MUST equal the persona directory name AND the channel-tag agent name used in canary-rings.json (e.g. 'murat', 'dev-lead').

name.description: Human display name (e.g. 'Murat'). For framework-backed personas this MUST match the locked framework identity.

title.description: Role title (e.g. 'Master Test Architect & Quality Advisor').

summary.description: One-sentence statement of what this persona is for.

status.description: Lifecycle stage. 'draft' = not yet cut; canary/ring0/ring1/stable mirror the furthest channel this persona has been promoted to; 'retired' = decommissioned.

owner.description: Owning team in 'org/team' form (matches CODEOWNERS). Team-based ownership only, per codeowners-standard.md.

definition.description: Where and how the persona is actually implemented. A persona MUST declare at least one layer. Per persona-standards.md §Definition, the manifest is the unifier ON TOP of these layers — it points at them, it does not replace them.

layers.description: Ordered list of definition layers that implement this persona.

kind.description: framework-agent: a vendored framework agent (BMAD etc.). copilot-profile: an agents/<name>.md profile. workflow-prompts: a prompts/<persona>/ library driven by a shell orchestrator. gh-aw: a GitHub Agentic Workflow .md compiled to a .lock.yml.

path.description: Repo-relative path to the layer's root (the SKILL.md dir, the profile .md, the prompts dir, or the gh-aw .md).

framework.description: Required when kind == 'framework-agent'. Records the upstream provenance and pin so the manifest can point INTO the framework rather than fork it.

vendor_pin.description: Vendored version/tag, MUST match the framework's VENDOR.md (e.g. 'v1.19.0').

skill.description: The framework skill/agent id backing this persona (e.g. 'bmad-tea').

local_overrides.description: Local customization applied on top of a vendored layer (never hand-edit vendored files). Overrides that would benefit upstream should be flagged for contribution back.

path.description: Repo-relative path to the override file(s) (e.g. a customize.toml overlay or an orchestration wrapper).

upstream_candidate.description: true if this override is a general improvement that SHOULD be proposed back to the upstream framework rather than kept local forever.

skills.description: Capabilities the persona can invoke, consumed by path (vendor-neutral). Advisory index for humans; the authoritative wiring is inside the definition layers.

triggers.description: The trigger matrix — the checklist of every surface this persona may act on. Per persona-standards.md §Triggers, default is advisory (read-only) everywhere; write access is opt-in per surface and MUST carry a gate label and a trust floor.

default_mode.description: The mode for any surface not explicitly listed. MUST be 'advisory' or 'off' — write is never a default, only an explicit per-surface opt-in.

opt_out_label.description: Label that opts an item out of this persona entirely (convention: '<id>:hands-off').

surface.description: The work-item surface / GitHub event family.

events.description: Specific event actions in scope (e.g. ['opened','labeled'] for issues).

mode.description: advisory: comments/reviews/labels only, no code changes. write: opens/pushes PRs or mutates repo content.

gate_label.description: REQUIRED when mode == 'write': the label that arms the persona on this surface (e.g. 'dev-lead').

trust_floor.description: Minimum author_association allowed to trigger on this surface. Defaults to trust.author_association_floor when omitted.

bridge.description: Optional repository_dispatch/workflow_dispatch bridge type when the surface cannot run the agent inline (e.g. 'discussion' events).

trust.description: Trust boundary applied before the persona consumes agent quota.

author_association_floor.description: The default set of author associations allowed to trigger the persona. Convention: [OWNER, MEMBER, COLLABORATOR].

runtime.description: How the persona is wired into CI. Optional for advisory-only personas that run entirely inside an existing workflow.

host.description: Repo that owns the reusable (matches canary-rings.json host).

reusable.description: Repo-relative path to the reusable workflow, if any.

caller_stub.description: Repo-relative path to the thin caller stub.

run_workflow.description: Display name of the workflow whose run history feeds the health gate. MUST match canary-rings.json run_workflow for this id.

permissions.description: The full permission set the reusable requests. The caller stub MUST grant a superset of this or the fleet gets startup_failure.

evals.description: The held-out eval gate. A persona MUST NOT reach the 'stable' ring without a passing eval set. See evals/README.md (dev/holdout split, holdout-guard.yml).

required_before.description: The ring by which the eval set must exist and pass. Recommended: 'stable' at minimum.

path.description: Repo-relative path to the persona's eval set (holds dev/ and holdout/ splits). Canonically 'evals/<id>/' so it inherits validate-cases.py and holdout-guard.yml.

min_cases.description: Minimum held-out cases required before promotion to required_before.

canary.description: Pointer to the single source of truth for ring rollout. This manifest MUST NOT restate rings, members, or gate knobs — only reference the registry entry by id.

registry.description: Path to the canonical ring registry (petry-projects/.github standards/canary-rings.json).

agent.description: The agents.<key> in canary-rings.json for this persona. MUST equal `id`.

references.description: Relevant docs, ADRs, or issues (e.g. the ADR that introduced this persona).

address.description: How humans @-mention this persona. Uses a GitHub team handle (never a user account) so mentions route to exactly one owner. Optional — omit for personas not yet wired for @-mentions. The team slug MUST equal the persona id (enforced by the persona validator in CI).

handle.description: GitHub team handle in org/team-slug form. The team slug must equal the persona id — the mention router resolves a mention by stripping the org prefix.

aliases.description: Additional team handles this persona answers to (e.g. carried over from a rename). Must not collide with any other persona's handle or alias.
